title: CBHC (Coldboot Haxchi)
requirements: A Wii U on the latest firmware (5.5.6 or lower in North America, 5.5.5 or lower elsewhere)
A legitimately purchased, digitally installed DS Virtual Console game from the eShop
An SD card formatted as FAT32 (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
The Wii U connected to the internet
warnings: CBHC is a legacy method with a !!HIGH brick risk!!. Aroma is the recommended way to hack a Wii U today.
The DS game !!MUST be legitimately installed!! from the eShop.
Don't format the system while CBHC is installed.
Don't delete the user account that bought the DS VC game.
Don't re-install the same game using WUP Installer or from the eShop.
Don't uninstall the DS VC game without properly uninstalling CBHC first.
Don't move the DS VC game to a USB drive.
Breaking any of these rules !!WILL brick your console!!. Make a NAND backup and don't skip it.
_note: Legacy CBHC method, transcribed from the old homebrew-guide (docsify) Wii U guide. Downloads come live from the linked GitHub releases and the fortheusers CDN.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**CBHC (Coldboot Haxchi)** extends a Haxchi install so CFW launches **automatically on every boot**, so no need to run the game each time. The trade-off is risk: installed incorrectly, CBHC !!**will brick your Wii U**!!.

Read the warnings on this guide carefully. CBHC requires a **legitimately installed, paid DS VC game**. Modern hacking uses **Aroma**, so only use CBHC if you have a specific reason.

Make sure your Wii U is on the **latest firmware** and your SD card is **FAT32** (verified below). Never name the SD card `wiiu`.

steps.title: Choose and buy a DS VC game
steps.body:
Pick a compatible DS Virtual Console game and buy it from the eShop as a **digital download**. It **must** be legitimately installed from the eShop because a WUP-installed copy !!will brick when CBHC runs!!.

If you don't already own one, **Brain Age** (North America) / **Brain Training** (Europe) is usually the cheapest.

Compatible games include: Animal Crossing: Wild World, Big Brain Academy, Brain Age / Brain Training, Donkey Kong: Jungle Climber, the Kirby DS titles, The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass / Spirit Tracks, Mario & Luigi: Partners in Time, Mario Kart DS, New Super Mario Bros., Pokémon Mystery Dungeon: Explorers of Sky, Star Fox Command, Super Mario 64 DS, Wario: Master of Disguise, WarioWare: Touched!, Yoshi's Island DS, and Yoshi Touch & Go.

steps.title: Put the files on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads the latest homebrew and unpacks each piece into the right folder on your SD card:

- The **Homebrew Launcher** and its installer payload
- **WUP Installer GX2** and the **Homebrew Launcher Channel**
- The **Wii U NAND Dumper** and the **Homebrew App Store** installer
- **Haxchi** and **CBHC**

This is everything the guide would normally have you download by hand. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS quietly scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii U homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Run the browser exploit
steps.body:
Put the SD card into the **Wii U** and make sure it's online.

Open the **Internet Browser** and go to `wiiuexploit.xyz`. Tap **Run Homebrew Launcher!** The console should boot into the **Homebrew Launcher**.

If the screen freezes or goes white, wait a few seconds; if nothing happens, reboot, clear the browser's saved data, and try again.

troubleshoot.label: I've got an error on the browser

steps.title: Make a NAND backup
steps.body:
!!This is critical!! for CBHC because it's your !!only way back from a brick!!. In the Homebrew Launcher, open **Wii U NAND Dumper**.

Set it up like the picture: **SLC: Yes, SLCCMPT: Yes, OTP: Yes, SEEPROM: Yes**. Leave **MLC** on **No** unless your SD card is bigger than the console's storage.

Press **A** to dump. When it finishes, power off the Wii U.

steps.title: Save your NAND backup
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of the Wii U and plug it back into this computer.

Press the button and Graphite grabs the backup the console just made (`slc.bin`, `otp.bin`, and the rest), copies it into a folder on your **Desktop**, and clears the files off the SD card to free up space. If a backup folder already exists, it saves a new one alongside it.

With CBHC's brick risk, !!keep this backup somewhere safe!!.

steps.title: Install Haxchi, then CBHC
steps.body:
Put the card back in the Wii U and run the browser exploit to reach the Homebrew Launcher. If you haven't downloaded your DS VC game from the eShop yet, do it now.

1. Open **Haxchi**, highlight your DS VC game with the D-Pad, press **A** to inject it.
2. Back at the Wii U Menu, launch the modified game, and the console reboots with CFW.
3. Launch the modified game **again holding A** to reopen the Homebrew Launcher.
4. Open the **CBHC** app, highlight the **same game**, press **A** to install CBHC.
5. Back at the Wii U Menu, **reboot**. If CBHC installed correctly you'll see a new boot menu.
6. With the D-Pad, set **Autoboot: Disabled** → press **A** until it reads **Autoboot: System Menu**.
7. Select **Boot System Menu** and press **A**, and the Wii U Menu launches with CFW.

Your console now boots into CFW automatically every time. Re-attach any USB devices.

steps.title: Install the Homebrew Launcher Channel (optional)
steps.body:
Adds a Homebrew Launcher icon to the Wii U Menu.

1. Power on (it autoboots into CFW). During the `Autobooting...` screen, press **Home** to reach the boot menu.
2. Select **Boot Homebrew Launcher**.
3. Open **WUP Installer GX2**, select `Homebrew Launcher Channel`, press **Install**, confirm **Yes**.
4. Choose **NAND** as the destination.
5. Press **Home** until you're back at the Wii U Menu, and the channel now appears there.

troubleshoot.label: The install failed or Data Management complains

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
CBHC is installed and your Wii U boots straight into CFW every time. Drop homebrew into `wiiu/apps` on the SD card.

Remember the !!brick rules!!, especially never uninstall or reinstall the injected DS game without **uninstalling CBHC first** (see the Uninstall legacy CFW guide).
